sol.raw_output: {"reviewer_model":"gpt-5.6-sol","verdict":"PASS","checks":{"direct_target_use_justified":true,"paired_causal_design":true,"generation_score_isolated":true,"same_writer_and_equal_budget":true,"safety_and_regression_gate":true,"no_overfit_or_unsupported_credit":true,"transport_and_cost_bounded":true,"best_practice_aligned":true},"blocking_issues":[],"minimum_changes":[],"rationale":"The frozen design supports a leakage-controlled paired comparison of the bundled clause-bound architecture against the production baseline. It fixes writer family, retrieval, inputs, temperature, and answer-token budget; requires replicated strict gains, no canonical losses, citation and conflict safety, bounded cost, dual semantic acceptance, and full regression before official credit or default-on activation."}

fable.raw_output: {"reviewer_model": "claude-fable-5", "verdict": "PASS", "checks": {"direct_target_use_justified": true, "paired_causal_design": true, "generation_score_isolated": true, "same_writer_and_equal_budget": true, "safety_and_regression_gate": true, "no_overfit_or_unsupported_credit": true, "transport_and_cost_bounded": true, "best_practice_aligned": true}, "blocking_issues": [], "minimum_changes": [], "rationale": "Direct use of the four frozen residual questions is justified: the residual set is frozen in S163, the treatment (S228 clause-bound architecture) pre-exists this experiment rather than being tuned against these targets, and official credit is gated behind dual semantic review plus the full regression suite. The paired design is sound: identical questions, 51 frozen chunks, fixed retrieval, same claude-sonnet-4-6 writer family at temperature 0 in both arms, two replicas per arm, and a strict causal gain definition (both treatment replicas cover, neither baseline replica does) that resists single-sample noise. Generation/score isolation holds: the generation packet contains only question and served context; the score packet opens only after all eight generations are sealed COMPLETE_SCORE_NOT_OPENED; planner-created obligation labels are explicitly untrusted generation outputs. Budget parity holds: 3,600 output tokens per baseline answer versus a 3,600-token aggregate writer cap per treatment answer, so decomposition cannot win via a larger answer budget; the haiku planner is part of the intervention, not the answer writer. Safety gates are adequate: no loss of any canonically covered obligation in either treatment replica, zero invalid citations, safe handling of the frozen conflict, and production stays default-off. Unsupported-credit risk is controlled: the deterministic match is explicitly a routing gate, 154/157 is labeled a projection, and score movement requires dual semantic acceptance plus regression. Transport/cost are bounded: SDK retries off, at most one manual retry on recognized transport errors only, zero retries for semantic/schema failures, sealed attempt logs, a doubled conservative preflight under $25, and exactly one Sol plus one Fable review with no convergence loop. Nonblocking observations: (1) claude-fable-5 serves as both a design reviewer and the independent semantic adjudicator; using a distinct model family for one of those roles would strengthen independence, though the tasks differ and dual acceptance plus regression bounds the risk. (2) Temperature-0 replicas from the same model are stability probes rather than fully independent samples; the strict-gain definition still functions as intended. (3) Regenerating the baseline afresh (rather than reusing archived outputs) is the correct choice here, as it controls for provider-side drift in the paired comparison."}
